what_it_does: Builds a machine-readable design DNA package by reading JSON sources from dna/, deriving DTCG-style tokens and component/template/layer artifacts, enforcing an axis-ramp lightness invariant, writing JSON outputs under factory/output/, and logging generated files plus sample OKLCH conversions.
observations: Runs as a Node CLI script with synchronous filesystem reads and writes.
Loads many dna/*.json inputs into named layer constants before constructing output artifacts.
Derives spacing, radius, and type token values from grammar constants instead of inline numeric literals.
Converts sampled hex colors to OKLCH metadata for color tokens, ramps, and warmth stops.
Emits tokens, templates, components, icons, application, vocabulary, modes, motion, sequence, voice, spaces, invariants, dials, and a manifest.
Checks that axis_ramp OKLCH lightness decreases monotonically before writing outputs.
The write target is factory/output/ while comments and logs describe a dist slice.
standouts: Design tokens preserve derivation metadata in $extensions.dna alongside concrete values.
The build step includes a perceptual color conversion path used by both tokens and dials.
The manifest records layer paths and generation inputs as part of the emitted package.
Several output objects carry prose descriptions that encode design constraints as data.
conventions: Uses ES modules and Node built-ins only.
Uses synchronous IO appropriate for a short build script.
Keeps output serialization deterministic with JSON.stringify(obj, null, 2) plus a trailing newline.
Uses DTCG-like token keys with $value, $type, $description, and $extensions.
Builds component ids by replacing underscores with hyphens.
Uses short uppercase constants for imported DNA layer payloads.
concerns: hexToOklch assumes a 6-digit hex color after removing # and does not validate malformed input.
The comments and console output refer to dist, but the out helper writes under factory/output/.
The build script mixes data loading, transformation, invariant checking, output schema assembly, and logging in one file.
The manifest version and notes are static literals that can drift from emitted layer content.
notes: This is a build-time compiler that turns design DNA source JSON into a structured factory output package with derivation metadata and invariants.
questions: Should the generated directory be named output or dist?
Are hex colors always guaranteed to be 6-digit sRGB values before reaching hexToOklch?
Is manifest.version intended to be updated manually or derived from source metadata?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it compiles a machine-ready design DNA pack from source JSON files, with the local caveat that the actual target directory is output rather than dist.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make design rules portable by compiling raw DNA sources into predictable JSON artifacts while preserving formulas, provenance, and enforceable invariants.
novelty: It treats design system artifacts as a compiled data package, including color-space metadata, narrative layers, voice rules, spatial faces, and executable lint-style predicates in one build path.
contribution: This file contributes a deterministic packaging layer that transforms source design substrates into serialized artifacts, keeps derivation and provenance attached to generated tokens, and records invariant checks that a fused system would retain as build-time validation.
summary_for_embedding: A Node build script that reads design DNA JSON sources, derives DTCG tokens and layered design artifacts, validates color-ramp lightness order, and writes a structured factory output package.
intention_for_embedding: It was for compiling design grammar, visual vocabulary, motion, voice, sequence, spaces, and invariants into a portable machine-ready package with derivation metadata.

symbols.does: Reads and parses a named JSON file from the dna/ directory.
symbols.description: This helper centralizes loading of source DNA layer files by name. It returns parsed JSON from dna/{name}.json using the build root computed from the current file location.

symbols.does: Creates an output subdirectory if needed and writes a pretty-printed JSON artifact.
symbols.description: This helper serializes an object to factory/output/{path}, ensures the parent directory exists, appends a trailing newline, and returns the relative output path for logging. It is the single write path used by the build list.

symbols.does: Converts a hex sRGB color string into a formatted OKLCH string.
symbols.description: The function strips the leading #, normalizes RGB channels, linearizes sRGB, applies OKLab conversion matrices, derives chroma and hue, and formats the result as oklch(L C H). It is used to attach perceptual color metadata and to check ramp lightness ordering.

symbols.does: Extracts one two-character hex channel and normalizes it to a 0-1 value.
symbols.description: This inner helper in hexToOklch reads two characters at a channel offset, parses them as base-16, and divides by 255. It assumes the cleaned color string contains standard two-digit RGB channels.

symbols.does: Converts an sRGB channel value to linear-light RGB.
symbols.description: This inner helper implements the standard sRGB transfer function branch: low channel values divide by 12.92, and other values use the 2.4 power curve. The linearized channels feed the OKLab conversion math.

symbols.does: Wraps a hex color in a DTCG-style color token with OKLCH and provenance metadata.
symbols.description: This helper produces {$value,$type,$description,$extensions} color objects. It records the color role when provided and defaults provenance to pixel-sampled source evidence.

symbols.does: Wraps a pixel value in a DTCG-style dimension token with derivation metadata.
symbols.description: This helper emits a dimension token whose concrete value is a px string and whose $extensions.dna records the formula or source derivation. When a measured value is supplied, it records that measured px value separately.

symbols.does: Maps a warmth anchor object into surface records containing hex and OKLCH values.
symbols.description: This helper transforms each named surface color in an anchor into an object with the original hex and converted OKLCH string. It is used to build the cool and warm stops for the warmth dial.
